entry.title: process approachfor: Empties Staging with EWM
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

in a process we need to deal with certain customer racks as empties/returnables. in these racks there is e.g. 102 lb. from production side we want to stage only full racks. what is the smartes way to handle this?

currently: we pack 1 pc of packmaterial inside a hu and trigger a staging with event-driven kanban and the help of a 1 pc product wt in order to bring a whole rack. this comes unfortunately with several downsides. e.g. you can't use bulk storage since here you need to deal with hu wt's or you can't use standard customizing to prevent mixed sotrage because the racks are always mixed since the packing material is part of the hu in order to not lose it.

 

any thought's?

thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: PMW with 4110 UTF-8 code page for arabic symbols disp...
entry.content:
Good day @Natalia_Sverchinskaya 

Thank you for your question.

When Arabic characters show as dots at the bank, it is almost always one of these two causes:

  • The file is not truly delivered as UTC 8 end to end or
  • The bank’s pain.001 implementation does not support Arabic characters in the Name fields, even if the file is UTF8.

You can pinpoint what you might have missed by:

First, confirming the XML is really UTF 8 and not only configured as 4110. For that generate the payment file in the Manage Payment Media, then open the XML in Notepad++ or any editor that has similar function. Check the first line contains something like: encoding= “UTF-8”. If you see UTF 8 in the header but the editor detects a different encoding, you have an encoding conversion in the download or transfer chain.

See https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1730272 for more details.

Second, verify that transfer channel is not converting your file. This could be how your email handles attachment, how you copy paste into blank portal, your middleware converting to ISO-8859- or Windows- encodings, your file being saved by your text editor as “ANSI”. Do not change from UTF 8 if you use BTP integration Suite or SFTP. See https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/avoiding-encoding-issues for more details.

Some banks accept technical accepts Arabic in the file but render unsupported characters as dots or reject them during validation. You can carry out a practical test by:

  • Putting an Arabic string into Remittance information and keep Dbtr/Nm in Latin only.
  • If remittance displays correctly but debtor names becomes dots, the bank’s restriction is one name fields, not on UTF 8 itself.

Finally, in some CGI XML formats, the following reference provides details on handling of special characters, which can impact what ends up in the output. You may need to align with your banks guidance if they expect specific handling rather than only changing code page.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1988783

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Reversal of partial settlement incorrect on account assignment for accrual reversal condition
entry.content:
Dears

I am facing a problem that the system does not reverse a partial settlement (or final for that matter) because of unbalanced document.

For a French Sales Org the original document is as Z* condition types copied from standard

Header: 

Item with accruals reversal:

Account assignment for this item

00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
00000001 Tax account will be determined for account key ESA
00000001 Tax account 0000015452 found and posted with posting key 50
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
00000001 Tax account will be determined for account key ESE
00000001 Tax account 0000015401 found and posted with posting key 40
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Vendor 0000100248 posted with reconciliation account and posting key 31
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCA2 ()
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, ZC1 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class
00000001 Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCA2 (Commission AccrRev)
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, ZC2 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCA2
00000001 Account 0000009696 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCA2)
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCS1 (External Commission)
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCS1
00000001 Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCS1)

The reversal document looks like:

Header:

Same item as before:

 

Account assignment for the same item including main messages with the error

FI/CO Interface: Balance in Transaction Currency 28.101,00- (EUR)
Document INT0000001 has status C and is complete
Chart of accounts SGL determined for company code FR01
The posting document is created with transaction RMRP (Incoming invoice)
_________________________________________________________________________
Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
Tax account will be determined for account key ESA
Tax account 0000015452 found and posted with posting key 40
_________________________________________________________________________
Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
Tax account will be determined for account key ESE
Tax account 0000015401 found and posted with posting key 50
_________________________________________________________________________
Vendor 0000100248 posted with reconciliation account and posting key 21
_________________________________________________________________________
Account determination for condition type ZCA2 ()
Account determination: acc.key, ZC1 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCA2)
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
Account determination for condition type ZCA2 (Commission AccrRev)
Account determination: acc.key, ZC2 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCA2
Account 0000009696 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
_________________________________________________________________________
Account determination for condition type ZCS1 (External Commission)
Account determination: acc.key, val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCS1
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCS1)

As you can see in the reversal - the red marked line is trying to post to the same G/L account with posting Key 50 (besides the 40) and because of this the document is unbalanced.

The settings for the condition contract are shared amongst several sales orgs and if I do the same for a German Sales Org (again same settings - same G/L accounts) the system reverts the document without any issue.

This occurs only in reversal of partial settlement and not in any other cases.

Any idea on how we can see why the system is trying to post with that other account key 50 besides de correct one of 40 (that reverts the original document posting)?

entry.title: How to use Expert Search for SAP Notes & KBAs in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Searching through countless SAP Notes and Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs) can feel overwhelming, especially when you’re looking for something specific. The good news? SAP for Me’s Expert Search feature makes this task much simpler. 

 

Before diving in, let’s clarify what SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs) are. 

  • SAP Notes describe a software issue and its solution. They include a description of the symptoms, the cause of the error, and the SAP release and support package level in which the error occurs. Depending on the type of error, an SAP Note may also include workarounds and links to support packages that solve the problem, and most SAP Notes contain coding corrections. 
  • SAP Knowledge Base Articles are practical "how-to" guides. Using business language, KBAs answer frequently asked questions arising from your daily work with SAP software. No coding corrections or modifications are provided. 

 

 Why Use Expert Search? 

The “SAP Notes & KBAs Expert Search” in SAP for Me provides an at-a-glance view on relevant updates, helps you organize your favorite notes, and allows you to retrieve notes based on advanced selection criteria, especially as a recurrent task.  This feature is extremely helpful if you carry out regular analyses of relevant notes.

 

Getting Started in 7 Simple Steps:  

  1. Login to SAP for Me with your S-user ID and password. 
  2. From the left-hand dashboard, navigate to Services & Support. 
  3. Click on the KBAs & Notes tab. 
  4. Select the “SAP Notes & KBAs Expert Search” card. 
  5. Click on the “Expert Search” tab.  

     

  6. Use the filter bar to specify your selection criteria. 
    • Example: Components (Starts): BC-DB*.  
    • As you begin typing the component key, the auto-completion feature will assist you in specifying the correct component. Only notes assigned to components that start with the entered key will be displayed. 
  7. Finally, click the “Go” button to run your search.  

 

Note: No special authorization is required in your S-user ID to use the Expert Search function in SAP for Me.  You can apply different filter criteria, such as Search Term, Software Component (Soft Comp), Component (Exact), and more. 

 

For more information, please refer to the Knowledge Base Article (KBA): 2388433- Information about using Expert Search for SAP Notes & KBAs - SAP for Me. 

 

Additional Resources:  

  • Knowledge Base Article (KBA): 2478289 - How to set up notifications for new or updated SAP Notes and/or KBAs with Expert Search filters - SAP for Me 

 

Got More Questions? 

 Please note that the Customer Interaction Center are always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have!    
 

If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A  All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community  

 

Don't Miss Out on Free Webcasts  

We would like to invite you to our free webcasts, which we believe can help you get the best from your support in SAP. You can find all upcoming CIC Webcasts. These sessions are open to all the users and will revolve around hot topics in support today such as SAP for Me, Case Management, etc.  

Additionally, you can explore a range of CIC how-to videos at learning.sap.com. Please feel free to share this information with your colleagues. We are looking forward to your attendance.  

 

Lastly, you may want to access all CIC Blogs here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/tag/CustomerInteractionCenter/tg-p/board-id/crm-blog-sap.  

Thank you for reading!  We hope you find this guide helpful.

entry.title: Re: Assignment Profile - Assign only to new job classific...
entry.content:
Hi Louis, could one option be to set up an assignment profile with the below rules:

  • Job Classification = [the job classification you're interested in]
  • User ID =/= [list of employees who currently have this job classification]

That way, it only picks up people who move into that Job Classification from now on, excluding the list of people who already have it. Although I always try to avoid creating APs with User IDs, in this scenario it sounds like it would be fine because it's just a static one-time list, rather than trying to capture a dynamic group of employees. 

If you were then to Download Preview Users, the expected result is that no users would be captured by this AP initially, until a 'new' person gets allocated to that Job Classification.

EDIT: I've just reread your scenario and on a second read it sounds like you're not talking about one specific Job Classification but instead about people moving from any Job Classification into another - in which case please ignore my answer!

entry.title: SAP TM Report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_BSG adaptation After System Copy or Refresh
entry.content:
SAP TM Report BSG adaptation After System Copy or Refresh

Introduction

Problem Overview

After performing a system copy or refresh in SAP environments, administrators frequently encounter critical issues where the "LOGQS" field in table "/SAPAPO/LOCMAP" continues to point to the source system instead of the target system. For example, the field may still reference "XXPXXXX100" (production) when it should point to "XXQXXXX100" (quality/test system). This misalignment disrupts location mapping functionality and can cause significant integration issues between ECC/S/4HANA.

The symptoms typically manifest as business partner errors, failed CIF integrations, and location master data inconsistencies that prevent proper supply chain planning operations. Users may experience "BP does not exist" errors when testing in a refreshed environment, even though the data exists but with incorrect logical system references.

Technical Background

The LOGQS field in /SAPAPO/LOCMAP represents the Business System Group (BSG) assignment for location master data in SAP Transportation Management systems. This field maintains the logical connection between source systems (ECC/S/4HANA), ensuring accurate data flow for supply chain operations.

During system copy or refresh operations, the location mapping table retains the original production logical system references, creating a mismatch between the copied data and the target system's expected logical system configuration. This occurs because standard system copies procedures focus on data replication but don't automatically adjust system-specific configuration fields like LOGQS.

Root Cause Analysis

The issue stems from the standard behavior of system copy operations, which replicate table contents without adjusting environment-specific logical system references. The /SAPAPO/LOCMAP table maintains BSG assignments that are environment-dependent, but these references aren't automatically updated during the copy process.

When production data is copied to quality or test systems, the BSG details reflect the production configuration while the target environment expects different BSG assignments. This creates a fundamental disconnect in the location mapping framework.

Step-by-Step Resolution Process

Pre-Resolution Checks

  1. Verify Current LOGQS Values: Access table /SAPAPO/LOCMAP via SE16N to identify entries with incorrect logical system references.

  1. Identify Target System Configuration: Determine the correct BSG and logical system values for the target environment
  2. Create System Backup: Ensure proper backup procedures are in place before making configuration changes

Correction Steps

  1. Access Resolution Tool: Execute transaction SE38 and run report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_BSG
  2. Configure Parameters: Set the old logical system (source) and new logical system (target) parameters in the report
  3. Execute Update Process: Run the report to systematically update LOGQS field values in /SAPAPO/LOCMAP
  4. Monitor Progress: Review the report output to ensure all relevant entries are processed correctly

Post-Resolution Validation

  • Integration Testing: Verify CIF connectivity between ECC/S/4HANAsystems functions properly
  • Location Master Verification: Confirm location master data displays correctly in transactions like /SAPAPO/LOC3
  • Business Process Testing: Execute end-to-end supply chain planning scenarios to validate complete functionality

Alternative Solutions

For environments requiring more comprehensive logical system changes, consider using BDLS (Logical System Name Conversion) which addresses logical system references across multiple tables, However, this approach requires careful consideration of all affected applications and thorough testing.

The report /SAPAPO/CHANGE_LOGSYS provides specific functionality for location master logical system changes, though it requires careful review of the source code comments to understand the full impact. Additional reports may be needed for related mapping tables like /SAPAPO/MATMAP, /SAPAPO/PPMMAP, and /SAPAPO/RESKEY.

Impact Assessment

Business Impact

Incorrect LOGQS field values can severely disrupt supply chain planning operations, causing failures in demand planning, supply network planning, and transportation management processes. Business users may experience system errors when accessing location-dependent functionality, leading to planning delays and operational inefficiencies.

Technical Implications

The misalignment affects DRF integration stability, potentially causing queue processing errors and data synchronization failures between integrated systems. System performance may degrade due to failed integration attempts and error handling overhead.

Prevention Strategies

System Copy Best Practices

  • Comprehensive Planning: Include logical system mapping updates in standard system copy procedures,
  • Documentation Requirements: Maintain detailed records of logical system assignments for each environment
  • Validation Checklists: Implement post-copy validation procedures that specifically verify LOGQS field accuracy

Monitoring and Maintenance

  • Regular Health Checks: Schedule periodic reviews of location mapping table consistency
  • Automated Monitoring: Implement monitoring solutions that detect logical system misalignments
  • Change Management: Establish procedures for managing logical system changes across system landscapes

Conclusion

SAP Note 3595300 addresses a critical post-system-copy issue that can significantly impact supply chain operations. The resolution involves using the dedicated report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_BSG to correct LOGQS field values in the location mapping table. Success requires careful planning, systematic execution, and thorough validation to ensure complete integration stability.

System administrators should incorporate these correction procedures into standard system copy workflows and implement monitoring mechanisms to prevent similar issues in future refresh operations. Proper attention to logical system consistency ensures reliable supply chain planning functionality across all system environments.

Refer:

  • SAP Note 3315995: Location: Report BSG adaptation
  • SAP Note 3396965: Changing Logical System (LOGSYS) for table /SAPAPO/LOCMAP
  • SAP Note 1909192: How to change variants of CIF integration model after system copy

Hope that can help you.

Thanks, and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud with Microsoft Po...
entry.content:
Hello @Mike_vanIngen 

Thank you for your question.

Signing in to Power BI’s OData Feed and organisational account is not a generic OAuth 2.0 client. It expects Azure AD style OAuth approach and specifically requires the service to return a WWW-Authenticate header that contains an authorisation URL posting to login-microsoftonline.com. If that header is missing or different, Power BI throws exactly that “The WWW-Authenticate header doesn't contain a valid authorization URL.” you see.

In Public Cloud, OAuth for inbound API access is designed for system to system OAuth patterns (comm arrangement OAuth details, token endpoint, scopes and in many cases OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertation or confidential client patterns), not for Power BI’s Azure AD specific organisational login flow. That explains why your basic Auth works, OAuth via Power BI OData fails. See https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-business-partner-oauthsamlbearer..html for more details.

Question: Are there specific prerequisites or configuration steps required?

Answer: You can configure OAuth on the S4H PC but Power BI OData Feed does not consume it unless the service behaves like Azure AD in the WWW-Authenticate challenge.

Question: Is SAP IAS officially supported for OAuth authentication with Power BI?

Answer: Not in the way Power BI’s organisational account expects. IAS is fine for SSO to SAP UIs but Power BI’s OData expects Azure AD endpoints and headers, not IAS

Question: Is this a known limitation of Power BI’s OAuth implementation?

Answer: Yes, Microsoft documents the exact WWW-Authenticate header pattern required for OData organisational sign in. If your service does not return this, Power BI won’t proceed. See Troubleshooting sign-in with OData feed for more details.

It is recommended to keep Basic Auth for OData. If your governance allows it, Basic Auth remains the most straightforward for Power BI OData Feed. You already confirmed it works.

Alternatively, you may consider using BTP as the integration layer, not direct Power BI OAuth. You can:

  1. Pull data from S4H Cloud APIs using BTP destinations.
  2. Land data in an analytics store, e.g., Datasphere, Azure SQL, etc.
  3. Connect Power BI to that store through Azure AD

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Is there a way to plan process orders in sequence by wk so that product A is made wk 1, prod b w2?
entry.content:
I work in a process industry where we have products that are "additive" for example, something like this:

Material A = Multi vitamin

Material B = Multi-vitamin + iron

Material C = Multi-vitamin + Iron and zinc

We would like to make these materials in sequence by week  For example:

Week 1 we make only Material A

week 2 we make Material B

week 3 we make material C

The reason is that if we make Material A, we can make material B without cleaning the equipment.  But if we make Material B, we cannot make Material A without cleaning the equipment because Material B has iron in it. It is the same with Material B and Material C.

We would like MRP to sequence these materials by week.  Weekly buckets don't work because it just buckets each material by week but doesn't separate them into different weeks.

Thank you so much for any replies.

entry.title: Re: Translation of User Defined Fields in B1
entry.content:
This is what I came up with. I'm a bit confused why the U_-prefix is not used thruout the system. Is there a table missing? It at least works as expected but the missing U_-part makes it look... hacky 😄

CREATE PROCEDURE "q_GetUserFieldTranslated"
/**
 * Return translatated user field based on Table and Language
 * Coded 2026-02-19 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
	IN  TableName     NVARCHAR(30),   /* B1 Table ID */
	IN  UserField     NVARCHAR(30),   /* User fields name without U_-prefix! */
	IN  UserValue     NVARCHAR(100),  /* Value to translate */
	IN  LangInt       int             /* B1 system language ID */
)
	LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
	SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
	tSQL              NVARCHAR(1000);

BEGIN
	
	tSQL := 'SELECT T0."Descr", '''|| :LangInt ||''' AS "LangInt", T3."Trans"
		FROM          "UFD1" T0
			LEFT JOIN "CUFD" T1 ON (T0."FieldID" = T1."FieldID" AND T0."TableID" = T1."TableID")
			LEFT JOIN "OMLT" T2 ON T2."TableName" = ''UFD1''
			LEFT JOIN "MLT1" T3 ON (T2."TranEntry" = T3."TranEntry" AND T3."LangCode" = '''|| :LangInt ||''')
		WHERE T0."TableID"  = '''|| :TableName ||''' AND T0."FldValue" = '''|| :UserValue ||'''
	';
  
  EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL;
  
END;

entry.title: OData v2 upsert on custom MDF Object creates record but does NOT trigger workflow
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm trying to create a new record in a custom MDF object (cust_mymdf) via the OData v2 API using a POST to the /upsert endpoint, with the goal of triggering the configured approval workflow.

What I'm trying to achieve:

  • Use the OData API to insert a termination request record as pending data.
  • Have the system's standard MDF workflow (configured on the object with a workflow group for approval) automatically trigger upon creation via API, so an approver receives a notification and can review/approve/reject.

Setup details:

  • Custom MDF object: cust_mymdf (effective-dated, externalCode as key field).
  • Pending Data = Yes on the object definition.
  • Workflow is configured directly on the object (have also tried business rule on save).
  • API user:   (non-admin/technical user, no "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission granted in RBP).
  • Authentication: Basic Auth (username (myuser@companyid) + password).

API call (via Postman):

  • Method: POST
  • URL:https://api55preview.sapsf.eu/odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true
  • Body (JSON):
{
  "__metadata": {
    "uri": "cust_mymdf(effectiveStartDate=datetime'2026-02-19T00:00:00',externalCode='19')",
    "type": "SFOData.cust_mymdf"
  },
  "externalCode": "19",
  "effectiveStartDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "externalName": "TestTerminate19Update",
  "cust_Employee": "B200394",
  "cust_TerminationDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "cust_OkToRehire": "Y",
  "cust_DeactivatePosition": "N"
}​

 

Result:

  • HTTP 200 OK
  • Response body:
{
  "d": [
    {
      "key": "cust_mymdf/effectiveStartDate=2026-02-19T00:00:00.000Z,cust_mymdf/externalCode=19",
      "status": "OK",
      "editStatus": "UPSERTED",
      "message": null,
      "index": 0,
      "httpCode": 200,
      "inlineResults": null
    }
  ]
}​
  • The record appears in Manage Data
  • No workflow is triggered — no approval request is created, no notification sent, nothing appears in Manage Workflow Requests or the approver's inbox.

    What I've already checked / tried:

    • The parameter ?workflowConfirmed=true is included in the URL (as required per sap documentation to simulate UI confirmation).
    • The API user does not have the "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission.
    • Manual creation via UI (Manage Data → Create new record for cust_MassTerminate) does trigger the workflow correctly (approver sees it).
    • Effective date is future-dated (2026-02-19), but same behavior occurs if I test with current date.
    • Workflow is set to trigger on create.
    • No duplicate pending records on the same key.

      According to SAP KBA 2396714 ("Workflow not Triggered via API or Integration Center") and community blogs (e.g., "Triggering workflows for MDF using OData API"), the combination of:

      • workflowConfirmed=trueand
      • API user without "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission

      should allow the workflow to trigger via API — but it's not happening in my case. Has anyone successfully triggered a custom MDF workflow via OData upsert in this scenario? Any additional prerequisites, payload tweaks, or known limitations I'm missing? Any help or pointers would be greatly appreciated — thank you!

I have tried Customer Hierarchy but that is also of no use eg.   1A Hierarchy is Amul India and 2A is Amul Jamnagar and Amul Pune.

Is there any standard solution for this OR what are the enhancements required to achieve this.

Someone please help me on this.

Thanks and Regards.

Saurav Mallick.

entry.title: Re: how use MDRO.SendLogViaEmail function sap c4c absl
entry.content:
Hi colleague,

The issue is likely related to the system sender configuration.

For MDRO.SendLogViaEmail() to work properly, a valid sender address must be configured, typically in the format donotreply@myXXXXX.mail.crm.ondemand.com

If the sender is not configured or enabled in the tenant, the email will not be sent even if the job finishes without errors.

I recommend checking the Outbound Email configuration in the system.

Best regards.

Fernando DJ Espitia.

entry.title: ICMR: Reconciliation balances do not match
entry.content:
Hi there,
has anybody any idea why there could be a mismatch between the view in the fiori "#reconciliation balances" and the detailed analysis. For the latter, I used the fiori manage machting documents.

In detail:
I want to check for  a specific consolidiation unit the reconciliation status and go to the fiori reconciliation balances, I filter on 012.2025 and my reconciliation case. It shows me the general status for one leading unit. Next, I pick a specific trading partner and see there are, for example 5.000 € difference YTD up to now as well as the same amount up to last matching. (see screenshot). We are in € the same time, currency translation doesn't play a role. 
When I cklick on the arrow, I cannot find the difference, of 5.000€ in manage assignments. Here there is only one unassigned item of 300€, all else seemd to have auto-matched already. More importantly, when I do a detailed analysis of matching documents of these two trading partners,  I can trace the one unassigned item of e.g. 300€ that showed up in manage assignments, everything else seems fine (ok, I exclude reason code S98 - balance carryforward, but that's seems sensible to me). So only the 300€ remain, I cannot find these 5.000€ at all. Moreover, the totals do not match with the total in the overview. The totals are a little higher. And yes, I picked the right filters (recon. cases, period, year, trading partners etc.)

Any explanations for that? Why does the reconcilaition balance show me completely different numbers than the detailed analysis? My hypothesis is: It shows an older status and does not refresh, this would also explain why we have now a total for both trading partners that is a little more than the total shown in the reconciliaton balance status. Any experience with that?
@HengyeZhu @vincezk

entry.title: How to Validate Business Partner’s CEP (Brazilian postal code) through Extensibility using ABAP
entry.content:
How to Validate Business Partner’s CEP (Brazilian postal code) through Extensibility using ABAP

SAP Solutions:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (SAP Cloud ERP).
  • ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse.

Third party API:

              This blog utilizes the API https://viacep.com.br/. This is a free-to-use API provided by a third party, thus is not under any SAP responsibility. Keep in mind that its usage for CEP validation is at your own risk and that you can use any API of your choice.

Knowledge Required:

  • How to connect ADT for Eclipse to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.
    • Connect to the ABAP System | SAP Help Portal
  • How to create an ABAP Class using ADT for Eclipse.
    • How to create a Class in ABAP 00 - 6 Steps to start | SAP Community
    • How to create a class in ABAP 00 - example code, with little typing. AdT is great! | SAP Community

Step I – Create an ABAP Class

              First of all, we need to create an ABAP class that makes the requests to the API. It should also handle the API response, which is in JSON format. Below, you will find a sample implementation of this class, but keep in mind that this is just an example and can be adjusted as needed.

CLASS zbl_cl_get_request DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_address_br,
             cep         TYPE c LENGTH 9,
             logradouro  TYPE c LENGTH 100,
             complemento TYPE c LENGTH 100,
             bairro      TYPE c LENGTH 60,
             localidade  TYPE c LENGTH 40,
             uf          TYPE c LENGTH 2,
             estado      TYPE c LENGTH 40,
             regiao      TYPE c LENGTH 20,
             ibge        TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             gia         TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             ddd         TYPE c LENGTH 3,
             siafi       TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             unidade     TYPE c LENGTH 50,
           END OF ty_address_br.

    TYPES: tt_address_br TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_address_br WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    CLASS-METHODS create
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_instance) TYPE REF TO zbl_cl_get_request.
    CLASS-METHODS get_data
      IMPORTING iv_postal_code TYPE string
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_data) TYPE ty_address_br .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ZBL_CL_GET_REQUEST IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_data.
    DATA ls_viacep TYPE ty_address_br.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_url(  'https://viacep.com.br'  ).
      CATCH cx_http_dest_provider_error INTO DATA(lo_http_dest_provider_error).
        "handle exception
        DATA(lv_http_dest_provider_error) = lo_http_dest_provider_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( lo_destination ).
      CATCH cx_web_http_client_error INTO DATA(lo_web_http_client_error).
        "handle exception
        DATA(lv_web_http_client_error) = lo_web_http_client_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.

    " Set HTTP Method (GET, POST, etc.)
    DATA(lo_request) = lo_http_client->get_http_request( ).
    DATA(lv_uri) = '/ws/' && iv_postal_code && '/json/'.
    lo_request->set_uri_path( lv_uri ).
    " Send Request
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_response) = lo_http_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>get ).
      CATCH cx_web_http_client_error INTO lo_web_http_client_error.
        "handle exception
        lv_web_http_client_error = lo_web_http_client_error->get_text( ).
        "handle exception
    ENDTRY.

    " Get Response
    DATA(lv_resp_satus) = lo_response->get_status( ).
    DATA(lv_resp_text) = lo_response->get_text( ).
    /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize(
      EXPORTING json = lv_resp_text
      CHANGING  data = ls_viacep
    ).
    rs_data = ls_viacep.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create.
    CREATE OBJECT ro_instance.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Step II – Enable this Class to be Used in Key-User Extensibility

              To use this class in the Custom Logic app inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the property “Use in Key User apps”, under “API State”, must be set as “Yes”. This must be done in ADT for Eclipse.

Step III – Implement the Custom Logic

  1. In the customizing tenant of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, go to the Custom Logic app (F6957) and click on the “Create” button.
  2. Choose the BAdI “Validate BP BADI” (ID: “CMD_VALIDATE_BP”).
  3. Give it a name and description.
  4. Open the implementation and click on the button “Open Code Editor”.
  5. Add the sample code below.
  6. Publish the code, then start testing.

Again, this is just an example and can be adjusted as needed.

DATA: ls_bp_address LIKE LINE OF it_bp_address,
           ls_message    LIKE LINE OF ct_validationmessage,
           lv_message    TYPE string,
          lv_postalcode TYPE string.
*Clearing the variants, just in case
CLEAR: lv_message, lv_postalcode.
*Fiiling ls_bp_address with it_bp_address data
LOOP AT it_bp_address INTO ls_bp_address.
*Attributing the value for lv_postalcode
  lv_postalcode = ls_bp_address-postalcode.
*Removing the '-' from the CEP
  REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF SUBSTRING '-' IN lv_postalcode WITH ' '.
*Checking if the BP country is Brazil
  IF ( ls_bp_address-country = 'BR' OR ls_bp_address-country = 'Brazil' ).
    DATA(lo_srv) = zbl_cl_get_request=>create( ).
    DATA(ls_data) = lo_srv->get_data( iv_postal_code = lv_postalcode ).
    IF ls_data IS NOT INITIAL.
*Cheking if the region from the CEP's code list is differnent from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-uf = ls_bp_address-region.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |Region informed: { ls_bp_address-region }. Region CEP: { ls_data-uf }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
*Cheking if the city from the CEP's code list is differnt from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-localidade = ls_bp_address-cityname.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |City informed: { ls_bp_address-cityname }. City CEP: { ls_data-localidade }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
*Cheking if the street from the CEP's code list is differnt from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-logradouro = ls_bp_address-streetname.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |Street informed: { ls_bp_address-streetname }. Street CEP: { ls_data-logradouro }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

Step IV – Transport

After testing, you can transport it.

  • Creating Extension Items and Transporting Them | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: KSeF implementation in CIM (DRC ↔ CIM integration for Poland)
entry.content:
Hi All,

This weekend, CIM introduced the option to connect DRC with CIM for Poland, enabling integration with KSeF (National e-Invoicing System).

As far as I can see, the only available documentation so far is here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/CENTRAL_INVOICE_MANAGEMENT/38e7dd38764346d995e098282320effc/7614cc7d448a48b89227dbfeb2b4b905.html?locale=en-US

I have a few questions:

  1. Are there any additional materials, guides, or implementation notes available (beyond the help.sap.com documentation)?

  2. Regarding “Preparing Master Data Files for Import” – do you know what the correct file format should be?

    • In the sample data and in the data structure documentation, the values differ.

    • For example, what should be maintained in the Exchange System field: KSeF or sKSeF?

entry.title: SAP Earns Silver Stevie® Award in “Best Predictive Customer Service Using AI”
entry.content:
We are excited to share that SAP has been honored with a Silver Stevie® Award in the category Best Predictive Customer Service Using AI. This recognition celebrates our work in applying AI‑powered predictive technologies to anticipate customer needs, prevent issues, and deliver proactive support.

 

What Are the Stevie® Awards?
The Stevie® Awards are among the world’s premier business awards, created in 2002 to recognize outstanding achievements of organizations and professionals around the globe. They have become one of the most coveted distinctions in business, with more than 12,000 nominations submitted each year.

They are often referred to as the “Oscars of the business world”, reflecting their reputation for honoring excellence and innovation across industries. [stevieawards.com] [businesschief.com]

 

Why SAP Was Recognized
Because of the high scores awarded by the judges, our nomination “Next‑Level Support: Leveraging AI to Predict and Prevent Customer Challenges” received the Silver Stevie® Award in the 20th annual Stevie® Awards for Sales & Customer Service. This category honors organizations that have most effectively used AI to anticipate customer needs and proactively deliver support.

Since July 2023, SAP has advanced its support model by introducing a preventive risk mitigation approach – a shift from resolving issues after they occur to predicting and preventing them before they impact customers. By analyzing system health, historical trends, and critical events, AI helps us identify risks early and alert customers ahead of time.

This approach delivers clear benefits:

  • Proactive prevention of disruptions during seasonal peaks and other business‑critical periods.
  • Improved user experience by identifying outdated components, such as browser versions, that could affect system access after updates.
  • Greater stability and trust through fewer customer‑initiated incidents and stronger business continuity.

 

Building on a Strong Track Record
This Silver award adds to recent recognitions, including:

  • Best Use of Predictive AI at the Enterprise AI for CX Summit 2025.
  • Bronze Stevie® Award for Best Use of Technology in Customer Service.

 

Looking Ahead
The award reinforces SAP’s leadership in redefining customer support with AI, automation, and predictive intelligence. We will continue expanding our AI‑driven insights and scaling preventive capabilities – helping customers operate with greater confidence, resilience, and efficiency.

 

Press play to learn how SAP Support brings innovation and reliability together - so you can move faster with confidence.

entry.title: Re: PMW with 4110 UTF-8 code page for arabic symbols displayed as dots in S4 Cloud Public
entry.content: Unicode is the best choice for mixing any kind of characters (Arabic, English, Chinese, etc.), and UTF-8, UTF-16LE, UTF-16BE, etc. are the possible encodings for Unicode, you may choose any UTF format that is supported end to end. If there's an issue somewhere with UTF-8, you must identify at which exact step during the transfer there is a conversion from UTF-8 to something else.

entry.title: Re: mm02 attachments in smartforms
entry.content:
DATA: lt_attachments TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF sofolenti1,
ls_attachment TYPE sofolenti1,
lv_objtype TYPE swo_objtyp VALUE 'BUS1001', "Material
lv_objkey TYPE swo_typeid,
lv_bin_data TYPE solix_tab,
lv_xstring TYPE xstring,
lv_bmp TYPE xstring.

"Material number padded
lv_objkey = |{ lv_matnr ALPHA = IN }|.

"Get GOS attachments for material
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_OBJECT_GET_ALL'
EXPORTING
object_type = lv_objtype
object_key = lv_objkey
TABLES
object_content = lt_attachments
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

"Pick first JPG/PNG attachment
LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment
WHERE file_ext = 'JPG'
OR file_ext = 'JPEG'
OR file_ext = 'PNG'.
EXIT.
ENDLOOP.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

"Read attachment binary
CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
EXPORTING
document_id = ls_attachment-doc_id
TABLES
contents_hex = lv_bin_data.

"Convert SOLIX to XSTRING
CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
EXPORTING
input_length = ls_attachment-doc_size
IMPORTING
buffer = lv_xstring
TABLES
binary_tab = lv_bin_data.

"Convert JPG/PNG → BMP (SmartForms compatible)
CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_BMP'
EXPORTING
input = lv_xstring
IMPORTING
output = lv_bmp
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

"Pass lv_bmp to SmartForm
CALL FUNCTION 'ZMM_XXX_SMARTFORM'
EXPORTING
iv_matnr = lv_matnr
iv_image = lv_bmp.

ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Data migration in maintenance weekend
entry.content:
Dear @pauldorn,

Point 1.

Yes normally this is exactly what uptime is for Manage Prices Sales is the standard Fiori app to create/edit/copy/delete (incl. mass maintenance) sales pricing condition records

Important: Even during uptime, SAP can enforce temporary locks for certain activities around the upgrade most commonly extensibility/transport-related. If you can open the app and save condition records you’re fine but if you see system/provider lock behavior you may need to pause until the lock is lifted

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3564099

So, finish the condition load well before 22:00 UTC and leave time to validate pricing

Point 2.

No. During downtime the tenant is not available for productive work no reliable logon / processing

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/understand-upgrading-and-patching-processes-of-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public/ba-p/13695428

And even more importantly around the upgrade window (including parts of the uptime phase) SAP explicitly restricts the two things you asked about:

  • Data migration

SAP states that during upgrade/maintenance periods the Migrate Your Data Migration Cockpit app is available in read-only mode and you can hit messages like maintenance event is in process so you should not plan migration loads in this window

SAP Note and SAP Community below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2996675

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/faq-on-upgrading-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13657921

  • Transports / Software Collections

SAP states:

  • Export during system upgrade: No
  • Import during system upgrade: No

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3126032

Also SAP notes you can encounter System is currently locked by the service provider for transport/extensibility activities even before the weekend upgrade window depending on SAP preparation steps

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3564099

Summary:

  • Use uptime for business activities like maintaining/loading sales pricing conditions in Manage Prices Sales, plus validation
  • Do not schedule Migration Cockpit loads or transport imports/exports during the maintenance weekend window assume they are blocked/read-only/unavailable until the upgrade is fully finished
  • Use downtime only for offline prep (files, checklists, reconciliations) not system actions

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Claude Code + Python + SAP GUI Scripting: Your AI Agent for Any SAP Transaction
entry.content:
I asked Claude Code:  "what is your opinion about the library? Can you create dynamically new scripts or steps to fulfill a lot of different actions in SAP GUI?"

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Good question. Let me give you an honest, technical assessment.

What the library does well

Navigation and field interaction — start_transaction, set_field, send_vkey, press_button cover probably 80% of what any SAP script needs to do. These are generic enough to work across any transaction.

Grid reading — grid_read_all with find_grid is the most valuable piece. ALV grids appear everywhere in SAP reporting, so this one function unlocks hundreds of transactions.

Screen exploration — explore_screen is a genuine capability multiplier. It lets you discover field IDs on unknown screens without manual inspection.

Helper patterns — run_transaction_report is a good abstraction. One function call can drive FBL1N, MB52, VA05, ME2M and many others with just different parameters.

Honest limitations

1. Dynamic script generation — partially yes, but with a catch

The library gives Claude (or any LLM) enough primitives to compose new scripts. If you describe a task in plain language, I can write a script using these building blocks. But there's a problem: field IDs are system-specific. The path wnd[0]/usr/ctxtFIELD_NAME can differ between SAP releases, customizations, and screen variants. I can write the logic, but I can't guarantee the IDs without seeing your specific system.

2. find_grid is brittle

It only tries 5 hardcoded paths. Many transactions use custom container names. If none of the 5 match, it returns None silently. A real dynamic approach would need recursive tree traversal.

3. No scroll/pagination support

grid_read_all reads only what's currently visible or loaded. For large result sets, SAP paginates the grid. Without scroll logic, you silently get partial data — this is a significant gap.

4. Table controls vs. GridView

table_read_cell exists but is basic. Many classic SAP transactions (MM03, XK03, FB03) use dynpro table controls, not ALV grids. These need different traversal logic.

5. No recording/discovery mode

SAP's own Script Recorder generates IDs automatically by watching your clicks. The library has explore_screen but no equivalent of "watch me do this once, then repeat it." That would be the real dynamic capability.

Can I create new scripts dynamically from this?

Yes, for a large class of problems:

  • Any report transaction with a selection screen + ALV grid output → I can write the script
  • Any transaction where you can describe the fields and steps → I can compose it from the primitives

No, reliably, for:

  • Complex multi-screen workflows (purchasing, goods receipt, FI posting) without first running explore_screen to discover actual IDs
  • Transactions with non-standard grid containers
  • Anything requiring pagination of large datasets

What would make it truly dynamic

The one addition that would change the game is a recursive screen walker that returns the full element tree of any screen. With that, Claude could:

  1. You navigate to a screen manually
  2. Call explore_screen_deep()
  3. Claude reads the tree, understands what's there, writes the targeted script

That would make the "describe what you want → get a working script" loop much more reliable. Right now explore_screen only goes 2 levels deep, which often misses elements inside nested containers.

Bottom line: The library is a solid, usable foundation. It can dynamically generate scripts for reporting/extraction tasks well. For complex transactional workflows, it needs either screen-specific knowledge upfront, or a deeper exploration capability to discover that knowledge at runtime.

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
Interesting discussion.

I agree the solution is not obvious, but if the scenario is "moderation team is working with limited resources and relying on their customers (?) to support internal moderating capacity", then the possible outcomes seem clear:

  • Good Outcome: Forum moderation keeps up with bad/inaccurate answers. The SAP moderation team deletes bad Gen-AI posts/answers and deals with repeat offenders, preventing them from contributing further. The community continues to be able to leverage Gen-AI content that is useful (as, when used responsibly, Gen-AI can certainly be an effective tool and work multiplier).

  • Bad Outcome: Forum moderation does not keep up with removing bad/inaccurate answers. LLMs begin scraping incorrect information from SAP forums, exponentially multiplying the problem over time and negatively impacting customer experience with SAP products. Forums become a minefield of right and wrong answers, increasingly less useful over time. To keep the forums useful, users are essentially forced to create a personal whitelist of trusted users for answers.

The variables that determine which of the above occur are "moderation capacity" vs "volume of bad answers"... and given the suggestions above of under-resourced moderation, I reluctantly agree with @Sandra_Rossi's belief that "Bad Outcome" seems more likely. In fact, from the linked answers @Sandra_Rossi shared, we're already seeing it, with people denying they used Gen-AI despite obvious unchecked errors and Gen-AI formatting, and no quick and effective action from the SAP moderation team in response (or worse, moderators accepting potentially incorrect answers on behalf of a question asker).

As Gen-AI will be scraping forum posts for future answers, it's critical to keep the "training data" (community posts) as clean and correct as possible. The greater the degree of error you have in the training data, the less reliable any Gen-AI model using data from here will be – including Joule, I'd assume?

Some suggestions for increasing effectiveness of limited moderation resources:

  1. Moderation Tool: Ban accounts and posts after violating the rules multiple times, but leave links to accounts/answers up, replacing them with a page saying "This post/account was removed for violating the following community rules: Validate Gen-AI answers for factual accuracy and/or disclose Gen-AI use." This will publicly communicate irresponsible/blind use of Gen-AI is not tolerated or valued here.

  2. Moderation Tool + Policy: For banned accounts/posts, set up an appeal process. While this may result in some false-positives (i.e. some useful posts are temporarily hidden, and get returned after manual review), it gives moderators a tool for hiding suspected bad content quickly until it can be properly vetted, and forces lazy/bad actors to put in more work and wait to restore their accounts and posts (disincentivizing the use of blindly reposting Gen-AI content or using bots).

  3. Moderation Policy: Clarify expectations and outcomes for the community. Announce a 3-strike policy for rule violations before an account is suspended from posting (or something similar), and require users to appeal to continue posting.

  4. Forum Feature: Before a user posts likely Gen-AI content, scan it and have a warning that pops up saying "Our system has detected the likely use of a Gen-AI tool in the creation of this post, but you have not added a Gen-AI tag. Do you certify that this was written only by you, without the use of a Gen-AI tool?"

As well, as this is generally a forum of professionals rather than the general public, hopefully we can consider that expectation of professional responsibility when making design choices in this space.

Dealing with irresponsible use of Gen-AI is an issue the entire internet is struggling with, so teams need to make the correct design choices before the issue becomes too difficult to solve as, without specific and intentional counter-measures, it will.

In the end, I greatly appreciate the hard work of the SAP moderation team, and really don't envy the challenge that this represents!

entry.title: ABAP SQL INSERT syntax Column &A of &B and the expression &C are not compatible for copy operations.
entry.content:
Hi, I've got this syntax error in my ABAP 7.58 SP 1 system:

          Column &A of &B and the expression &C are not compatible for copy operations. (message number INSERT 203 in table TRMSG)

at the line 7:

SPFLI-CONNID is of type NUMC 4 in the ABAP Dictionary.

What's the problem, how to fix it?

Thanks.

Sandra

NB: here's the code as text:

REPORT zdemo.

INSERT spfli ##LOGGING_VERSUS_FROM_SELECT[SPFLI]
       FROM (
       SELECT 'Z1'                       AS carrid,
              CASE WHEN connid <> '0400'
                   THEN connid
                   ELSE '0399'
              END                        AS connid,
              countryfr,
              cityfrom,
              airpfrom,
              countryto,
              cityto,
              airpto,
              fltime,
              deptime,
              arrtime,
              distance,
              distid,
              fltype,
              period
         FROM spfli
         WHERE carrid = 'LH' ).

entry.title: Re: ABAP SQL INSERT syntax Column &A of &B and the expression &C are not compatible for
entry.content:
The ABAP documentation of INSERT ... FROM ( SELECT ... ) says:

The columns assigned to each other must have the same type properties with respect to built-in data type, length, and number of decimal places, with the following exceptions: [...] All other types must be exactly the same. This applies specifically to NUMC and RAW, where the lengths must match. The different categories of strings cannot be combined either.

In the line 7, CONNID is fine (NUMC 4), but '0399' in the line 8 is technically speaking of type CHAR 4.

CHAR 4 has a "greater value range" (*) than NUMC 4, so the data type of the CASE expression is CHAR 4 which is not the type of the target column in the target table (SPFLI-CONNID of type NUMC 4).

(*) ABAP Documentation of sql_exp - sql_simple_case :

The result has the dictionary type of the specification with the greatest value range.

One workaround is to use a host expression @( ... ) to convert the constant '0399' in the right type NUMC 4 (NB: CONV can be used instead of EXACT if you prefer):

...
              CASE WHEN connid <> '0400'
                   THEN connid
                   ELSE @( EXACT spfli-connid( '0399' ) )
              END                                         AS connid,
...

entry.title: Re: How to enable Joule for the SAC Tenant Owner in S4 pu...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Can you please check whether the following information solves your issue?

  • Ensure that the SAC tenant owner has the necessary roles assigned. Even though the owner might not have roles by default, assigning roles like BI Admin or Admin might be necessary for accessing certain functionalities. For more information, see SAP Analytic Cloud Integration with S/4HANA Cloud - SAP Community

  • Double-check the user provisioning settings. It seems like you have already tried scheduling user and group provisioning. Ensure that the provisioning job is correctly configured to include all necessary users, including the tenant owner. For more information, see Avoid access issues during the initial set up of SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP Community.

Kind regards, Henrike
